steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. In this ayah, Allah is making Eve as a mate for Adam.
Basic preposition meaning 'from'
Added هَا which refers back to the soul (نَّفْسٍ) mentioned earlier in the verse

steps.m: to join things together as pairs

steps.n: Added fatha and sukun to make the basic noun meaning 'spouse' or 'mate'. This word is being acted upon by جَعَلَ (made/created).
Added هَا at the end to show it belongs to 'her' (referring back to نَّفْسٍ - the soul). The fatha before هَا shows this word is being acted upon.
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
Adding أَ makes the verb causative - instead of something coming down by itself, someone is causing it to come down. This is a verb, so it doesn't take case endings like nouns do.
The و connects this to the previous actions Allah did: He created you (خَلَقَكُم) and made (جَعَلَ) your mate, and sent down livestock
Added fatha to show the vowel sound

steps.m: for/to you all (receiving أَنزَلَ - He sent down)
steps.n: Added كُم at the end to show this is directed to multiple people. This word shows who receives what was 'sent down' (أَنزَلَ) in the ayah.
Basic preposition showing source or origin
Added fatha at the end to connect smoothly with the next word (الْأَنْعٰمِ)
Added shadda on م for emphasis and smoother pronunciation with the previous word
Basic past tense verb form with fatha vowels
Forms a plural noun referring to animals that provide sustenance, showing why this root about blessing/provision came to mean cattle

steps.m: the cattle (that Allah sent down as mentioned in أَنزَلَ)
steps.n: Added 'the' at beginning and kasra at end. This word connects to أَنزَلَ (He sent down) earlier in the verse, showing these are specific cattle that Allah provided.
This is the basic root meaning related to value or price
Root letters connected with short 'a' vowels (fatha) to make the noun meaning 'price'
These are different words - 'thaman' means price while 'thamaani' means eight. They just look similar but aren't related
Adding ة makes this the feminine form of the number eight, which is used with masculine nouns in Arabic
Adds the fatha ending because this word is being acted upon by the verb أَنزَلَ (He sent down)

steps.m: to pair or match things together
steps.n: Basic root meaning related to pairs or coupling
Root meaning 'pair' becomes a noun meaning 'spouse/mate' by adding vowel marks
Changed to plural by adding أَ at start and ا after و to make 'many pairs/spouses'

steps.m: of pairs/kinds (in this ayah: eight kinds of cattle)
steps.n: Added ٍ (small marks under) to show this word connects to مِّنَ (from/of) before it, like saying 'of pairs' instead of just 'pairs'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changes past tense to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: he creates/is creating you all (object of the creation)
steps.n: Adds كُمْ to show who is being created - you all are the ones being created
This is a preposition that shows location or containment. In this ayah, it specifically shows that the creation happens inside the wombs.
Root related to inner parts or hidden things
Adds vowel marks to the root letters to make the basic word for 'belly'
Changes vowels to damma and adds ون to make the plural form
Adds kasra at the end to show this word is possessed by or connected to something after it (in this case أُمَّهٰتِكُمْ - your mothers)

steps.m: to be a source or origin
steps.n: Root related to leadership and guidance
The ُ (damma) makes it into a noun meaning the source/origin
This is a special plural form for 'mother' that adds ه before the plural ending ات
Adding ِ shows this word is owned by or connected to something else (in this case, 'your'). The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) but pronounced the same.
Adding كُمْ shows this belongs to multiple people being spoken to (you all)

steps.m: to create or form something from nothing
steps.n: Basic root meaning of creation
Connects the letters and adds vowel marks to turn the action into a noun meaning 'creation'. This is related to يَخْلُقُكُمْ (He creates you) in this ayah.
The ًا (tanween) at the end makes this a direct object - something that is created. It shows humans are being created by Allah.
Basic preposition showing where something comes from or what it's made of
The ن in مِنْ changes to match the first letter of the next word, shown by the shadda (ّ). This helps the words flow together smoothly when speaking.
Added vowel marks (fatha and sukoon) to the root letters to make the word mean 'after'
The kasra ending shows this word belongs to or is connected to the word خَلْقٍ (creation), making it mean 'after creation'

steps.m: to create, form, or shape something from nothing

steps.n: Changes from action to the noun form showing the process itself

steps.m: a creation (being acted upon in this ayah)
steps.n: Adds tanween kasra to show this is being acted upon in the sentence. This word appears in the phrase 'creation after creation' describing how Allah creates you in stages.
This is a basic preposition that shows something is located or happening inside something else. In this ayah, it shows that the creation happens inside the wombs.
Changed the verb into a noun meaning the state of being dark
Changed to plural form by changing the middle vowel
The word changed to show multiple layers of darkness by adding the feminine plural ending -aat. The -in at the end shows it comes after the word 'in/fee'
Adding vowel marks to make the word readable
Adding alif makes it a specific number word
Added tanween kasra to show this number is describing the word ظُلُمٰتٍ (darknesses) in the ayah
The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) to show we're pointing to something far away instead of close by
Added لِ to make the word stronger and more emphatic when pointing to something

steps.m: that (far, with extra emphasis, speaking to multiple people) (points to اللَّهُ in next ayah)
steps.n: Added كُمُ at the end to show we're speaking to multiple people. This word points to Allah (اللَّهُ) who is mentioned right after this word in the next part of the ayah.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Added the U mark at the end to show it's in the nominative case

steps.m: to care for, nurture, and help grow/develop
one who cares for and nurtures completely (subject of ٱللَّهُ)
steps.n: The doubled ب shows this is someone who nurtures perfectly and completely. This word refers to Allah (ٱللَّهُ) in this ayah.

steps.m: your (all of you) Lord and Nurturer (subject of ٱللَّهُ)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show this Lord belongs to or takes care of all the people being spoken to. The ُ before كُمْ helps connect the parts smoothly.
This is a preposition that shows something belongs to someone or is for someone
Added the pronoun 'him' (هُ) to show who something belongs to. In this ayah, it's referring to Allah (ٱللَّهُ) having ownership of something.

steps.m: to rule, govern, or have authority

steps.n: Changed the verb into a noun that means the place or domain where someone rules

steps.m: the kingdom, the sovereignty (belongs to اللَّهُ)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific and ُ to show it belongs to Allah in this ayah. This word is connected to لَهُ (to Him) in the ayah.
This is the basic form of the negative particle that denies or negates what comes after it
The fatha mark shows this is a strong 'no' - like saying 'absolutely not!'
The alif is written with a madda mark (آ) to show lengthening. This word negates إِلٰهَ (deity) in the phrase لَآ إِلٰهَ (there is no deity).

steps.m: to worship or be devoted to

steps.n: Changes the first letter to إِ and adds vowels to create a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'
Adds a small alif (ٰ) after the second letter to elongate the vowel sound

steps.m: any god (object of لَا - there is no)
steps.n: Adds fatha at the end because this word is the object of لَا (no/not) in the phrase لَا إِلٰهَ (there is no god)

This is a basic exception particle that shows something is different or excluded from what came before. In this ayah, it's excluding هُوَ (He/Him) from other possible gods mentioned in لَآ إِلٰهَ (there is no god).

steps.m: he (subject of the sentence, referring to اللَّهُ)
steps.n: This is a personal pronoun that refers back to Allah mentioned in the previous part of the ayah (ٱللَّهُ). It's used to emphasize that He alone is being discussed.
This is a question word asking about manner, place, or time. In this ayah, it's questioning how people could turn away after all the signs mentioned before.
The فَ shows this question follows from the previous statements about Allah's creation and power. It expresses amazement at how people could turn away after such clear signs.
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
Changed vowels to show passive voice - something being done to someone
Changed from past to present tense by adding تُ prefix, still means 'being turned away'
Added ون to change from singular 'you/she' to plural 'you all' - shows many people being turned away
This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition. Here it connects two ideas: IF you disbelieve (تَكْفُرُوا), THEN Allah is free from need of you.
Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed vowels and added ت to show it's happening now and you're doing it
Added وا to show many people are being talked to. This word comes after إِن (if) in the ayah, showing a condition.
This is a basic emphasis word that makes statements stronger, like adding 'indeed' or 'verily' in English
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from the previous statement about disbelieving (تَكْفُرُوا)
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. The shadda shows the doubled ل sound.
Added fatha at the end because Allah is the object of إِنَّ (indeed/verily). Also changed alif to wasl-alif since it comes after another word.

steps.m: to be without need of others

steps.n: Connected the letters with vowels to make a past-tense verb

Added tanween (double dammah) to make the word indefinite
This is a helper word (preposition) that shows movement or separation from something. Think of it like drawing an arrow pointing away from something.

steps.m: away from you all (object of غَنِىٌّ/free from)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show this applies to multiple people being talked to. In this ayah, Allah is saying He is free from (غَنِىٌّ) needing you all.

steps.m: and (connects this part to the previous statement)
steps.n: This word connects two parts of the sentence together
Adding لَا makes this a negative statement, showing that something does not happen. In this ayah, it's saying something is not pleased/acceptable.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Added يَ at beginning to show present tense, and changed middle vowel to sukun to match present tense pattern

Changed final و to ى with elongation mark (ٰ) because it's at the end of the word. This is a common change for words ending in و. In this ayah, this verb is connected to الْكُفْرَ (disbelief) which is what Allah is not pleased with.
Added kasra at the end to show this word is owned by or connected to something else that comes after it
Added لِ at the beginning to show these servants are being discussed as the recipients or beneficiaries of something

steps.m: for His servants (referring to Allah's servants)
steps.n: Added هِ at the end to show these are specifically Allah's servants, connecting back to the word الله mentioned earlier in the verse
Changed the root into a noun by adding ُ on first letter and ْ on second letter to show the concept itself rather than the action
Added ال at beginning to make it specific and َ at end to show it's being acted upon by the verb يَرْضَى (he is pleased with)
Adding 'and' at the beginning
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
The تَ prefix shows it's 'you' and the middle vowel change shows it's happening now
The وا suffix changes it from singular 'you' to plural 'you all'
The root letters form a present tense verb 'he is pleased' by adding يَ at the start and changing و to ى at the end
The ى at the end is dropped before adding pronouns

steps.m: he is pleased with it (referring to being grateful)
steps.n: The هُ shows what he is pleased with - in this case referring back to being grateful mentioned in the ayah

Added كُمْ to show this affects multiple people being spoken to. This whole word works with يَرْضَىٰ (He is pleased) to say who He is pleased with.
This is a negative particle that negates the verb يَرْضَىٰ (He is pleased with)
The و connects this phrase to the previous statement, showing these are related thoughts. It negates the verb يَرْضَىٰ (He is pleased with).
This root relates to carrying or bearing a burden
Command form - drop the و and add a kasra (ِ) under ز to say 'you carry!'
Added تَ at beginning to make it 'she' in present tense, and ُ at end to show she is doing the action
This is the root meaning of carrying or bearing a burden
Added alif after first letter and kasra on middle letter to show who does the action
Added ة at the end to make it feminine
Added tanwin (double damma) to show this is indefinite - meaning any bearer, not a specific one
This is the basic root meaning showing the concept of carrying or bearing a heavy load
Changed the verb into a noun by adding the vowel pattern ِ-ْ. This pattern often shows the result of an action.
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being carried in the phrase 'no bearer bears a burden'
This root carries the basic meaning of something coming after or being delayed
Changes to feminine form meaning 'another' or 'other'
The small alif (ٰ) is added above the ى to help with pronunciation
The madd symbol (ٓ) extends the sound for emphasis
Basic particle that shows one thing happens after another
Added marks (ُ and َّ) to show this means 'then' - the marks help us read it correctly

The small alif (ىٰ) at the end is just a writing style variation that doesn't change the meaning. In this ayah, it's showing direction towards 'your Lord' (رَبِّكُم).

steps.m: to nurture, care for, or look after
steps.n: The doubled middle letter (shadda) makes this an intensive form, emphasizing the complete nature of the nurturing
Changed from basic root meaning 'nurture' to a noun meaning 'complete nurturer' by joining letters and doubling the middle letter
Added kasra (ِ) to show this word is possessed by or connected to something else
Added كُم to show the Lord belongs to all of you (plural)
This is the basic concept of returning or going back
Adding مَ at the start and changing the vowels creates a noun showing where or when the return happens
Adding ُ shows this is the subject doing something in the sentence
Two changes: 1) Adding shadda on م shows emphasis, 2) Adding كُمْ shows it belongs to multiple people being spoken to
This is the basic concept of informing or announcing news
Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger, showing this is important information being shared
Changed to present/future tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Added كُم to show who is being informed (you all)

steps.m: and he will inform you all (about ما كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ)
steps.n: Added فَ to connect this to the previous statement, showing this happens as a result
This is a preposition that shows how something is done or connected

steps.m: with what (you used to do يَعْمَلُونَ)

Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
The middle letter و drops out because it's a weak letter. The first vowel changes to damma and the middle letter gets sukun.
Added تُمْ at the end to show this is talking about multiple people being addressed (you all)
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Added ت at start to make it 'you', and changed the pattern to make it present tense (happening now)

Adding هُ means 'he/it'. Here it refers to Allah being knowing (عَلِيمٌۢ) in the next part of the ayah.
Adding vowels makes this a past tense verb meaning 'to know'
Changed from past tense verb 'knew' to a special form meaning 'one who knows deeply' by adding ي
Added tanween to show this is describing Allah's attribute of complete knowledge

The بِ adds the meaning of 'with' or 'by', while the kasra at the end (ِ) shows this word is connected to what comes after it (الصُّدُورِ meaning 'the chests/hearts'). Together they mean 'by what is in the hearts'.

steps.m: chest, breast (the front part of the body)

steps.n: Changed the vowels and added و to make it plural, showing there are many chests
Added ٱل (the) at the beginning and doubled the ص with shadda

steps.m: of the chests (possessed by ذَاتِ - what is within)

Adding the conjunction particle wa (and) to the conditional particle idha (when)

steps.m: he was deeply touched/affected (subject of دَعَا)
steps.n: The doubled س (shown by ّ) makes the meaning stronger, showing this wasn't just a light touch but something that really affected the person

steps.m: to be close or familiar with something

Added tanween (double damma) to show this is any general hardship, not a specific one

steps.m: to call upon, invite, or pray to
he called upon/prayed to (subject of مَسَّ)
steps.n: Added fathas to make it past tense. This shows the person praying to their Lord after being touched by hardship.
The و at the end changes to ا because it's a weak letter at the end of the word

steps.m: to care for, nurture, or develop something completely
master who nurtures and cares completely
steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who does the nurturing action perfectly and completely
Adding هُۥ shows this Lord belongs to him. In this ayah, this word is what is being called upon when he says دَعَا (he called)

steps.m: to return or go back repeatedly

steps.n: The root letters were connected with vowels (َ ِ) and a ي was added to make it mean 'one who keeps returning'

steps.m: turning back to God (describing the person in دَعَا)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this describes the person who called out (دَعَا) in this verse
This is a basic preposition showing direction or destination. In this ayah, it connects to the verb يَدْعُوٓا۟ (he calls/invokes)
The ى changes to ي to prepare for attachment. This is just a spelling change, not a meaning change.

steps.m: to him (object of يَدْعُوٓا۟ - he calls/invokes)
steps.n: Added هِ at the end to show 'him'. In this ayah, this tells us who is being called upon or invoked.
This is a connecting word that shows one thing happens after another, with some time passing in between. The ُ (damma) and ّ (shadda) make the word stronger.

steps.m: to give or grant something valuable

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Adding shadda doubles the middle letter و to show the giving was done generously or abundantly

steps.m: he granted him generously (referring to the human who was given a blessing from Allah)
steps.n: Adding هُ shows who received the generous granting. In this ayah, it refers to the human who was given a blessing after being in distress.
Changed the root letters into a noun by adding the vowel kasra (ِ) and sukoon (ْ) marks
Added ة to show this is one specific blessing

steps.m: a blessing (as the object being bestowed)
steps.n: Added tanween (ً) to show this is one blessing (not specific which one)
This is a preposition that shows where something comes from or the source of something
The shadda emphasizes the م, making the meaning stronger

steps.m: from him (referring to نِعْمَةً - a favor)
steps.n: Adding هُ (meaning 'him/it') to مِنْ shows what the 'from' is referring to - in this case pointing back to the favor mentioned
Added vowels to make past tense verb. This word is the subject of يَدْعُوٓا۟ (he calls/invokes) in this ayah.
This is already in the correct form - no changes needed from previous step

steps.m: what (he used to call upon/invoke before)
steps.n: This is a relative pronoun that connects to يَدْعُوٓا۟ (he used to call upon/invoke) in this ayah. It refers back to whatever the person used to pray to or call upon before receiving the blessing.

steps.m: he was (describing what happened before يَدْعُوٓا۟ - he calls upon)
steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two vowels in past tense. This is a common change in Arabic that makes the word easier to pronounce.
Added vowels to connect the letters. The و changes to ا at the end because it's a special type of word.
Added يَ at the beginning to show it's happening now. The middle letter gets a sukun (ْ) and the last vowel changes to ُ. The ا changes back to و.
Added extra marks (ٓا۟) that help with pronunciation but don't change the meaning. In this ayah, this word is connected to what comes after it - the person is calling to something.
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or destination. Since it's a preposition, it doesn't come from a root.
The ى changes to ي before adding pronouns. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Added هِ at the end to show this means 'to him'. In this ayah, 'him' refers back to رَبَّهُ (his Lord) who the person is turning back to.
This is a preposition that shows something coming from somewhere or someone. In this ayah, it connects the word نِعْمَةً (favor/blessing) with هُ (him), showing the blessing is from Him.

steps.m: to be in front of or before something

steps.n: Changes from verb meaning 'to be before' to noun meaning 'before'

steps.m: to make something into something else, to set up, to put
he made/set up (something into something else)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past by one person

steps.m: and he made/set up (أَندَادًا rivals for اللَّهِ Allah)
steps.n: Added 'and' at the beginning. This word connects to أَندَادًا (rivals) which comes after it, showing what he set up, and اللَّهِ (Allah) showing who the rivals were set up against

Adding shadda on ل makes this the proper name for the one true God
Added لِ at beginning to mean 'for/to' and ِ at end to show this is the object being affected by the verb جَعَلَ (he made)

steps.m: to oppose or rival something by making yourself equal to it

steps.m: rivals/equals (being set up as rivals to Allah)
steps.n: The tanween with alif (ًا) shows this word is receiving the action of جَعَلَ (he made/set up). So the full meaning is that someone is setting up these rivals to Allah.

steps.m: to wander off the right path
he wandered off the right path

steps.m: he made (someone) wander off the right path

steps.m: he makes (someone) wander off the right path

steps.m: so that he makes (someone) wander off the right path

steps.m: so that he makes (someone) wander off the right path
steps.n: Added shadda on ل for emphasis and pronunciation

steps.m: away from (connected to سَبِيلِهِۦ - His path)

steps.m: to enjoy or benefit from something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added ت at beginning and doubled the middle letter to show doing the action to oneself intensively
Changed the final vowel to sukun to make it a command

steps.m: to reject, deny, or be ungrateful

steps.n: Changed from verb (action) to noun (thing) by adding marks above and on the letters

Added كَ at the end to show this belongs to the person being spoken to
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Forms an adjective meaning 'having the quality of being little/few'

steps.m: for a little while (describing how long to تَمَتَّعْ/enjoy)
steps.n: This word describes how long the person should enjoy - for a little while. It's connected to the command تَمَتَّعْ (enjoy) in this ayah.
This is a special word that makes what comes after it more important and certain

steps.m: indeed you (being addressed by قُلْ - say!)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show this is talking to one male person. This connects to the command قُلْ (say!) earlier in the verse.

steps.m: from (connecting أَصْحٰبِ with what comes before)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition showing where something comes from or what group it belongs to. In this ayah, it connects أَصْحٰبِ (companions/people) with what comes before, showing they are 'from' that group.

steps.m: into broken pieces (what the plants are turned into)

This is a special word that adds emphasis to what comes after it. In this ayah, it emphasizes that there is definitely a reminder/lesson in what was mentioned before.
This is a basic word that shows location or being inside something. It helps connect words by showing where something is, like saying 'in the house' or 'within the city'.
The ي changes to ى at the end of words in Arabic writing. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning. In this ayah, it shows where Allah placed the water - in the earth.
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun used to point to things that are close by
The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) called an overbar, which doesn't change the meaning
Adding لِ changes it from pointing to something near to pointing to something far away

steps.m: that (far) (pointing to the previous examples in the ayah)
steps.n: The كَ is added to complete the word. In this ayah, it's pointing to the examples Allah just gave about rain, plants, and their lifecycle
Changed from basic root meaning 'remember' into the noun 'remembrance' by adding vowel marks ِ and ْ
Added ى at the end to make it mean 'something that causes remembrance'
Added لَ at beginning to emphasize that this is definitely/surely a reminder
This is a special word used to refer to people who possess certain qualities. It doesn't come from a traditional root pattern.

steps.m: for the owners of (connected to ذِكْرَىٰ - reminder)
steps.n: Added لِ at the beginning to show this word is receiving the reminder mentioned earlier in the ayah

steps.m: to understand deeply, to be wise
steps.n: The root refers to the core or essence of something, especially wisdom and understanding
Adding the damma (ُ) creates a noun meaning the essential part of something
Changes from single 'core/mind' (lubb) to plural 'minds' by adding alif at start and expanding middle - like how many languages make words plural by adding letters

steps.m: the minds/intellects (possessed by أُو۟لِى - those who have)
steps.n: The word is made definite with ال and the kasra ending shows it belongs to أُو۟لِى (those who have) in the phrase أُو۟لِى ٱلْأَلْبٰبِ (people of understanding)
Added fatha to show this is asking about who does something
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from something before it

steps.m: Then is the one who (subject of شَرَحَ)
steps.n: Added أَ at the beginning to make this a question. This word starts a question about the person who Allah has opened their heart to Islam (mentioned in شَرَحَ that follows)

steps.m: to expand or open up something (especially the heart/mind/chest)

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show Allah did this action in the past. This word is followed by صَدْرَهُۥ (his chest) showing what was expanded.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' by adding vowel marks
The word for 'god' becomes the special name 'Allah' by adding ال with unique pronunciation, doubling the ل sound, and adding ُ to show Allah is doing the action
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make the basic noun. This word is what's being expanded in the phrase 'Allah expands his chest.'
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being acted upon by the verb شَرَحَ (expanded)
Added هُۥ at the end to show this is 'his chest'. This is what Allah expands in the phrase 'Allah expands his chest.'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make basic past tense verb
Changed verb into noun meaning 'the act of submitting'
Added both 'for' (لِ) and 'the' (ال) at the beginning, with kasra at the end to show connection
Combines 'then' (فَ) with the pronoun 'he' (هُوَ)
Basic preposition showing something is on top of or upon something else
Added alif khanjariyah (small alif) which helps with pronunciation but doesn't change the meaning. This word connects to نُورٍ (light) in the ayah, showing someone is 'upon light'.
Changed from action (shining) to thing (light) by adding damma on first letter and sukun on middle letter
Added tanwin kasra at the end to show this is an indefinite noun (like saying 'a light' instead of 'the light'). In this ayah, it's describing the light that comes from Allah.
Basic preposition meaning 'from'
The shadda (ّ) on the م adds emphasis to show importance or connection to what follows

steps.m: to nurture, care for, and guide to completion

steps.n: Added fatha and shadda to create the noun form showing someone who does this action completely

steps.m: one who nurtures and guides (possessed by something)
steps.n: Added kasra to show this word is being possessed by something else
Added هِ to show it belongs to him. This word is possessed by نُورٍ (light) in the phrase 'light from his Lord'
The word already has the required shadda on ب, so no additional changes needed
Added vowel marks to show pronunciation - fatha on و and sukun on ي
Added tanween damma to show this is a general concept, not referring to a specific instance of woe
Added فَ at beginning to connect this warning to the previous statement, showing this is a consequence
Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense
Changes from verb 'became hard' to feminine adjective 'hard'
Added 'the' to specify which hardened thing we're talking about
Added 'for/to' at the beginning and a mark at the end to show it follows 'for'
Added emphasis mark (shadda) on ل because it comes after another ل in the ayah
Forms the singular noun 'heart' - named this way because ancient Arabs saw the heart as something that constantly turns over emotions and thoughts
Changes the internal vowels and adds و to make it plural. This is called a 'broken plural' because it breaks apart the original word structure to make it plural instead of just adding an ending

steps.m: their hearts (the hearts of those who are hard-hearted, mentioned in the previous part of the ayah)
steps.n: Adds هُم at the end to show these hearts belong to them. The ُ before هُم helps connect it smoothly to the word
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what it's made of. Here it connects 'light' (نُورٍ) to 'his Lord' (رَّبِّهِۦ)

steps.m: from (connects رَّبِّهِۦ to نُورٍ, with emphasis)
steps.n: The shadda (double letter mark) makes the 'م' stronger to emphasize the connection
Changed the verb into a noun by adding kasra on first letter and sukun on middle letter
The kasra at the end shows this word belongs to Allah (اللَّهِ) in this ayah
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word in Arabic that always refers to the One True God.
Added ِ at the end to show possession, since this word owns/possesses 'remembrance' (ذِكْرِ) in this ayah. Also added shadda on ل and changed initial alif to wasl form.
Added و to make the word stronger for pointing to things far away
Added ٰٓ (alif with madda) to show even more distance

steps.m: those (pointing to something far) (subject of فِى ضَلٰلٍ مُّبِينٍ)
steps.n: Added ئِكَ to show we're pointing to multiple things. This word is the subject of the phrase 'in clear error' that follows it.
This is a basic preposition that shows location or being inside something. In this ayah, it shows that the disbelievers are in a state of misguidance.
The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.

steps.m: to wander off the right path

steps.n: Changed from root letters to a noun meaning 'going astray' by adding fathas between the letters
Adding alif makes the meaning stronger - like saying 'really lost' instead of just 'lost'

steps.m: a complete loss of direction (after في)
steps.n: The word got a tanween (double dots) at the end because it's describing being 'in' a state of being lost
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Changed from verb 'made clear' to noun meaning 'one who makes clear'
The doubled م adds extra emphasis to show something is very clear
The tanween kasra (ٍ) shows this word is describing the 'light' (نُورٍ) mentioned earlier in the ayah
Changed the root into a noun form meaning 'one who is worshipped'
This is the unique name for The One True God in Islam. The ال makes it definite (like 'the'), the shadda emphasizes the ل, and the ُ shows this is the subject of the sentence.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb

steps.m: he sent down (subject of أَحْسَنَ, referring to اللَّهُ)
steps.n: The shadda shows Allah did this action with special care and attention, sending down the revelation gradually and repeatedly. This word connects to اللَّهُ (Allah) who is doing the action, and أَحْسَنَ (best) which describes what is being sent down.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: he made excellent/perfect (describing نَزَّلَ - sent down)
steps.n: This word describes how Allah sent down the best speech/discourse. The أ at the beginning makes it mean 'to make something excellent' rather than just 'to be excellent'
Root ح-د-ث became the noun حَدِيث (speech/narrative) with kasra at the end to show it belongs to أَحْسَنَ (best)
Added ٱلْ at the beginning to make it specific. This word is possessed by أَحْسَنَ (best/most beautiful), forming the phrase 'best of the narrative'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed the vowel pattern and added alif to make it into a noun. This word is what's being sent down in the ayah.

steps.m: a book (object of نَزَّلَ - sent down)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this word is the direct object (the thing being sent down) in the sentence. It is receiving the action of the verb نَزَّلَ (to send down).

steps.m: to look like or match something

steps.n: The ا shows two things are similar to each other
Added تَ at start to show things becoming similar to each other (mutual action)
Added مُ at the start to make it mean 'something that matches' instead of 'it matched'
Added emphasis mark (shadda) on م
Added ًا at the end to show this word is describing something else in the sentence
Added مَ at the start to make it mean 'things that are', and added ا and ي at the end to show many repeated things

steps.m: things that are repeated (in accusative case)
steps.n: Added a fatha mark at the end to show this word is being acted upon in the sentence
This is a quadrilateral root (4 letters) that describes the physical sensation of shivering or getting goosebumps
Adding vowels to make the basic past tense form of this 4-letter verb
Added تَ at beginning to show present tense with feminine subject (referring to جُلُودُ/skins in the ayah). Changed last vowel to ُ to show present tense.
Added shadda on final letter to show intensity of the trembling. This describes how the skins of those who fear Allah tremble when hearing His words.
This is the basic preposition meaning 'from'

steps.m: from it/him (referring to كِتٰبًا - the Book)
steps.n: Added the pronoun هُ which refers back to the Book (Quran) mentioned earlier in the verse
Changed the action into a physical thing that has that quality
Changed from single skin to multiple skins using broken plural pattern
Added ُ to show this word is doing the action in تَلِينُ (become soft)
Base form of the relative pronoun that connects descriptions to people or things
Added ٱلْ at the beginning and doubled the ذ to make it specific
Added ينَ at the end to show we're talking about multiple people. This word is the subject of يَخْشَوْنَ (they fear) in this ayah.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show past tense meaning
Changed from past to present by adding يَ at start and changing the ending to ى - this is how verbs ending in ي change in present tense

steps.m: they fear (referring to those who fear Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: Added plural marker ون (which becomes وْنَ) to show multiple people doing the action. The ى changes to و because of the weak letter rule.

steps.m: one who nurtures completely (object of يَخْشَوْنَ)
steps.n: The doubled middle letter (shadda) makes this a noun for someone who does the action perfectly and completely. This word is what the people fear/revere in the ayah.
Adding هُم shows this Lord belongs to or is connected to them. This is what they fear/revere in the ayah.
This word connects two actions in sequence - first they fear (يَخْشَوْنَ), then something else happens. The ُ (damma) and ّ (shadda) are essential parts that make the word complete.
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
Added تَ to make it present tense for 'she/you (feminine)'
Added vowel marks (fatha and sukun) to make the root into a simple noun meaning 'skin'
Changed middle and end vowels to 'u' sound to make plural, like many Arabic nouns
Added ُ to show this is the subject doing the action of trembling (تَقْشَعِرُّ)
Added هُمْ to show these are the skins of those who fear their Lord
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to create the singular noun 'heart'
Changed vowels to damma and added و to make it plural, showing multiple hearts
Adding the conjunction prefix و meaning 'and'

steps.m: and their hearts (subject of تَلِينُ - becomes soft)
steps.n: Added the possessive pronoun suffix هُمْ (their) to indicate masculine plural possession. This word is the subject of تَلِينُ (becomes soft) in this ayah.
This is a preposition showing direction or destination. In this ayah, it connects to the word ذِكْرِ (remembrance) to show movement or direction towards Allah's remembrance.
Added an alif symbol (ىٰ) at the end to show proper pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: remembrance (the act or thing being remembered)
steps.n: Changed from an action (remembering) to the thing itself (remembrance)
Shows this word is connected to the word Allah (اللَّهِ) that comes right after it in the ayah, forming the phrase 'remembrance of Allah'
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word in Arabic that always refers to The One True God.

Added kasra at the end to show this word is being possessed by or connected to something else (in this case, it's connected to the word ذِكْرِ meaning 'remembrance'). Also added shadda on ل and changed initial alif to wasl form.
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun for pointing to something nearby
The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) to show distance
Adding لِ makes it point to something even farther away

steps.m: that (pointing to Allah's Book mentioned before)
steps.n: The كَ at the end makes it specific to what was just mentioned - in this case, referring back to Allah's Book (the Quran) that was described in the previous part of the verse

steps.m: to guide or show the right way

steps.n: Adding ى at the end makes it a complete noun meaning 'guidance'
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ل, making this the unique name for The One True God
Added kasra at the end to show this connects to the word ذِكْرِ (remembrance), meaning 'remembrance of Allah'. Also changed initial alif to wasl form since it comes after another word.
Added vowels to connect the letters and make the past tense form
Added يَ at start to show present tense, changed middle vowel to sukun for lightness, and last vowel to kasra to match present tense pattern
Added ى at end to show it's a present tense verb that ends in a long vowel
Added kasra to show this is a preposition meaning 'with'
Added هِ which means 'him/it' to show what the preposition 'with' connects to
Added the small و (ۦ) to help with pronunciation. This word refers back to Allah's guidance (هُدَى) mentioned just before it in the ayah.
This is a relative pronoun used for people. In this ayah, it refers to the person that Allah guides, connecting to the verb يَهْدِى (He guides).
The middle letter ي changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This is a common change in Arabic that makes words easier to say.
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and ُ at end. This word is connected to the previous phrase about Allah's guidance, showing He guides whoever He wishes.
Added long alif to show this is an ongoing or repeated action of wanting/wishing
This is a question word that became a conditional word meaning 'whoever'. It connects to the next word يُضْلِلِ (leads astray) as its subject.
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it

steps.m: he went away from the right path
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb

The base word 'went astray' changes to 'makes someone go astray' by adding يُ at the start. The ending ِ shows it's connected to 'whoever' (مَن) in the sentence. It's about Allah choosing who to guide or not guide.
Changed the root into a noun form meaning 'one who is worshipped'
Added the u-mark at the end to show it's in the nominative case
Added 'the' to make it specific/definite
Added fatha to show this connects what comes after it to what came before. Here it connects to the verb يُضْلِلِ (he leads astray).
Added مَا which means 'not' to create a negative connection. Together فَمَا means 'then not' or 'so not', connecting to يُضْلِلِ (he leads astray).
Added fatha to the letter lam to show it's a preposition meaning 'for' or 'to'
Added هُ at the end to show who the action is for. This هُ refers back to ذِكْرِ ٱللَّهِ (remembrance of Allah) mentioned earlier in the ayah.
Added the small و (ۥ) after هُ as a pronunciation helper to show the 'u' sound is extended slightly

This is a preposition showing that the shivering/trembling happens because of or as a result of something. In this ayah, it connects how the skins (جُلُودُ) tremble (تَقْشَعِرُّ) because of or from what follows (هُ - referring to the Book).

steps.m: to guide or show the way

steps.n: Added vowels to make it past tense 'he guided' - like adding -ed in English
Changed from verb 'guided' to noun meaning 'one who guides' by adding alif

steps.m: a guide (after مِنْ in this ayah)
steps.n: Added tanween kasra to show it's indefinite and comes after the word مِنْ (from/of). The ي drops when tanween is added.
This particle turns a statement into a question, like adding a question mark in English
The فَ shows this follows from something before it, like saying 'then' or 'so'

steps.m: Then is the one who (protects with his face from يَتَّقِى)
steps.n: The مَن means 'the one who' and connects to the verb يَتَّقِى (protects) that comes after it

steps.m: to protect or guard against harm

steps.n: Connected the root letters و-ق-ي with vowels to make the word 'he protected' in past tense
Added اِتْ at the beginning to show doing the action to yourself (like saying 'self-protect' instead of 'protect')
Changed to present tense with يَ prefix and changed voweling

steps.m: he protects himself (against سُوٓءَ ٱلْعَذَابِ - the evil of punishment)
steps.n: The ت and ق merged with a shadda, showing extra care in protecting oneself (specifically from punishment mentioned in the ayah)
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make the noun for 'face'
Added بِ at beginning to show this face is being used as a means or instrument (in this case, using one's face to shield from punishment)

steps.m: with his face (object of يَتَّقِى - shields/protects)
steps.n: Added هِۦ at the end to show this is 'his face'. This word is being used to explain how someone tries to shield themselves from punishment (يَتَّقِى) on the Day of Judgment.
Root meaning 'bad' became a noun meaning 'badness' by adding the damma vowel mark
Added a fatha to show this word is receiving the action - it's the bad thing someone is being protected from

Adding ٱلْ makes it refer to a specific punishment rather than punishment in general
The ِ shows this word is being acted upon by سُوٓءَ (evil) in the phrase 'evil of the punishment'
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make the basic noun meaning 'day'

steps.m: day (object of يَتَّقِى - what is being protected against)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this word is receiving the action from the verb يَتَّقِى (protects against) in this ayah
Changes from basic root to noun meaning 'the act of standing'
Adding ة makes it refer to one specific instance - in this case, the final resurrection.
Adding ال makes it refer to the specific, known resurrection.
The kasra at the end shows this connects to another word, and the alif is written as a long ā.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense form
Changed the vowels to show this happened to someone/something rather than someone doing it
Added و at the beginning to connect this with what came before it

steps.m: to do wrong or act unjustly

steps.n: Changes from verb (to do wrong) to noun (wrongdoer)
Changed from singular (one wrongdoer) to plural (many wrongdoers) by adding ين at the end. The ظ got stronger (doubled) in pronunciation. The word shows who is being talked to.
The definite article ال makes the noun definite
Adds the preposition لِ meaning 'to/for'
Changed to command form by adding damma (ُ) on first letter and sukun (ْ) on last letter - like saying 'you taste!'
Added وا at the end to make it a command for multiple people
This is a basic particle that connects what comes before with what comes after. Here it means 'that which' because it's referring to the deeds they used to do.
Added fatha mark to show proper pronunciation. This word is the object of ذُوقُوا (taste), meaning 'taste that which you used to earn'.
Added damma on first letter and dropped the weak letter و to form the basic command. This is a common pattern with hollow verbs (verbs with و in middle).
Added تُ at the end to change from a command ('be!') to past tense ('you were')

steps.m: you all were (referring to the wrongdoers mentioned as الظَّالِمِينَ)
steps.n: Added مْ to make the verb plural. This word refers back to 'the wrongdoers' (الظَّالِمِينَ) mentioned earlier in the verse.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Added تَ at start to show 'you' are doing it, changed vowels to show it's happening now

steps.m: you all gain/earn (referring to the wrongdoers/ظَّالِمِينَ in this verse)
steps.n: Added ون at the end to show many people are doing it. In this verse, it's referring to what the wrongdoers (ظَّالِمِينَ) were earning through their actions.
Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past

Added shadda (double mark) on the middle letter to show the denial was strong or emphatic. This word is connected to فَأَتَىٰهُمُ (then came to them) in the ayah, showing what happened before the punishment came.
Added ن to make it refer to multiple people instead of just one person
Added ٱلّ at the beginning to point to specific people. This word refers to previous deniers mentioned in كَذَّبَ (they denied)
This is a basic preposition showing where something comes from or the starting point of something. In this ayah, it shows the starting point in time - 'from before them'
Added vowels to make a noun meaning 'before' that can show when something happened

steps.m: before them (refers to those who denied the truth)
steps.n: Added ِهِمْ at the end to show this refers to 'them' (the previous deniers mentioned in the ayah)
Added fatha marks and changed ي to ى because it's at the end. This word refers to العَذَابُ (the punishment) which is what came to them.
Added فَ to show this happened after they denied (كَذَّبَ) in the previous part of the verse

steps.m: then it (the punishment) came to them
steps.n: Added هُمُ to show who the punishment came to. The ٰ (alif khanjariya) is added to show the long vowel sound more clearly.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changes from the verb 'to punish' into the noun 'punishment'. This is not the doer - it's the thing that happens. In this ayah, punishment is what came to them.
Adds 'the' at the start and ُ at the end to show this word is doing the action (it is the subject of أتى/came)
Basic preposition showing where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects with قَبْلِهِمْ (before them) to show the timing started from before them.
This word is used to talk about a place or location

steps.m: from where (connects to مِنْ in this ayah)
steps.n: Added vowel marks to make it into a word that shows where something happens. In this ayah, it works with مِنْ (from) to show where the punishment came from.
This is a negative particle that makes the verb يَشْعُرُونَ (they perceive) into 'they do not perceive'
Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changes to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Makes it plural to show many people are doing the action. This word comes after لَا (not) in the ayah, so it means 'they do not perceive'
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
Adding أ at the start makes someone cause the action to happen to someone else
Adding فَ shows this happened as a result of something before it

steps.m: and so He made them taste (subject is اللَّهُ)
steps.n: Adding هُمُ shows who received the action - in this case 'them'. Allah is the one doing the action.
Changed from a verb root meaning 'to worship' into a noun meaning 'god' by adding special vowel marks
Added ال to specify this is THE one true God, and the hamza merged with the lam creating a doubled letter
Added a helper alif to make it easier to start saying the word
Added damma to show this is the subject (doer) of the verb أَذَاقَ (made them taste) in this ayah
Added vowel marks to make it a noun meaning the result of disgrace
Added ال to make it specific (this particular disgrace)

steps.m: the disgrace (object of made them taste)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end because this disgrace is what Allah made them taste (object of فَأَذَاقَهُمُ)
This is a preposition that shows where or when something happens. Here it shows where Allah made them taste humiliation.
The ي at the end is written as ى when it's at the end of a word. This doesn't change the meaning.
Added vowel marks (fatha) to root letters to make the basic word for 'life'
Added و and ة to change from the basic word for 'life' to mean a specific life or instance of living
Added ال to make it definite (specific life)

steps.m: of the life (possessed by/connected to something)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end because it follows the word في (in/during) in the ayah
Changed the root letters to make a word for something that is close or lower compared to something else. In this ayah, it refers to the life that is closer/lower compared to the afterlife.
Added 'the' at the beginning and doubled the د letter to show it's a specific lower life being talked about
Added vowels to connect the root letters together
Long ا after second root letter makes this a noun meaning the result of punishment. This word is in a possession relationship with الآخرة (the Hereafter), meaning 'the punishment of the Hereafter'.
ل adds emphasis, like saying 'surely' or 'indeed'
Adds conjunction 'and'

steps.m: and surely the punishment (subject of أَكْبَرُ)
steps.n: Added ُ to show this is the subject (doer) of the verb أَكْبَرُ (is greater) in this ayah
Changed the root into an active participle (doer) by adding alif and vowels to mean 'the one who/which is last'

steps.m: the life that comes last (the Hereafter)
steps.n: Added ة to change from 'last' to mean specifically 'the Hereafter' (the final life after this world)
Added ال to make it specific and ِ at the end because it's possessed by the word عَذَابُ (punishment) in عَذَابُ ٱلْءَاخِرَةِ (punishment of the Hereafter)
Added vowels (fatha, damma, fatha) to make it mean 'it became great' in the past tense
Added أ at the beginning and changed vowels to make comparative form meaning 'greater/bigger'
Added ُ to show this is the subject of لَعَذَابُ (punishment) in the ayah

steps.m: if only (they knew what Allah had planned)
steps.n: This is a particle that expresses a wish or regret about something that didn't happen. Here it shows regret that people don't realize what Allah has planned for them.

Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb

steps.n: Added plural marker وا to show multiple people doing the action. This verb acts on شُفَعَآءَ (intercessors) in this ayah.

This is a basic preposition showing where something comes from or showing separation. In this ayah, it connects the word شُفَعَآءَ (intercessors) with دُونِ (besides/other than), showing the intercessors are being taken from besides Allah.
The kasra (ِ) under the م shows this word is connecting to what comes after it. It's like saying 'from' before starting a new idea.

steps.m: His promise (which He fulfilled for us)
steps.n: Added هُۥ at the end to show this is Allah's promise that He fulfilled, as mentioned in the ayah
The word وَعْد (promise) has هُ (his) added at the end to mean 'his promise'. The small و mark in the first version is just a writing style that means the same thing.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added 'أ' at the start changes meaning from 'inherited' to 'made someone inherit' - like giving inheritance to someone else
The نا ('na') at the end changes the meaning from 'he made inherit' to 'he made us inherit' - it shows we are the ones receiving the inheritance

steps.m: and he made us inherit (object of قَالُوا - they said)
steps.n: The و connects this to what was said before. This whole phrase is what 'they said' in the ayah.
Added short 'a' and sukoon marks to show how to pronounce 'earth'
Added ال to make it definite and fatha at the end to show it's the object being inherited in the phrase 'made us inherit the earth'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Added ت at start and doubled middle letter to show doing action to oneself
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and ُ at end
Changed from 'he' to 'we' by replacing يَ with نَ
This is a preposition that shows something comes from somewhere or is part of something. In this ayah, it connects to الْجَنَّةِ (the Garden/Paradise) to show where they are taking places from.
Added a fatha (َ) at the end to help connect smoothly to the next word الْجَنَّةِ (the Garden/Paradise). This is like adding a connecting sound to make the words flow better when reading.

steps.m: to cover or conceal something completely

steps.n: Added vowels (fatha) and doubled the middle letter (ن) to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he covered'
Added ة to make it a noun referring to an enclosed space, which came to mean 'garden' since gardens were typically enclosed
Added ال to specify this particular garden/paradise

steps.m: of the garden (location where people will dwell in مِنَ)
steps.n: Added kasra to show this is connected to the word مِنَ (from) before it
Adverb of place
The damma ending makes it a complete adverb of place
This word shows where something happens - in this case where they will settle in Paradise. The ُ ending is always there, it's not added.
Added vowel marks (fatha) between the root letters to make it mean 'he wanted' in the past
The weak letter ي changes to ا, which is a common change in Arabic verbs
Added نَ at beginning to show 'we' and changed the end vowel to ُ to show present tense
Added alif with madda (آ) to show long vowel sound
Changes to a special verb used only for praising things

steps.m: and how excellent! (praising أَجْرُ ٱلْعٰمِلِينَ - the reward of the workers)
steps.n: Adds فَ to connect this praise with the previous statement about Allah fulfilling His promise. The word is praising the reward mentioned right after it.

steps.m: to pay back, to give compensation

steps.n: Root letters got vowels to make a simple noun meaning 'reward'
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject being praised in the phrase 'how excellent is the reward'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Added alif after first letter and kasra under middle letter to show who does the action
Added ين to make it plural (more than one worker)
Added 'the' (ال) at the start to make it specific, showing these workers belong to/are connected to أَجْرُ (reward)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added ت at beginning to show 'you' and changed vowels to show present tense. The ي at end changes to ى because it's at the end of the word.
Added و with fatha at beginning to connect this to what comes before. This verb connects to 'the angels' (ٱلْمَلٰٓئِكَةَ) which comes next in the ayah.
Adding the vowel marks makes it a complete past tense verb
These are different words - 'malaka' means 'to own' while 'mala'ik' comes from 'malak' meaning angel. They just look similar but aren't related.
Adding ة at the end makes it refer to all angels as a group
Adding ال makes it definite, referring to all angels as a group

steps.m: the angels (as the object being seen)
steps.n: Adding fatha at the end shows this is the object of the verb 'you see' (تَرَى)

steps.m: to surround or go around something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added alif after first letter and shadda on final letter to show someone who does the action
Added kasra at the end to show it's being used as an object
Added ين to make it plural - means multiple people doing the action
Added case ending to complete the word's grammatical function
This is a preposition that connects the angels' surrounding (حَآفِّينَ) with what they are surrounding (حَوْلِ - around). It shows where the action starts or comes from.
Changes verb into a noun meaning 'around'
Added kasra at the end to show this word is possessed by العرش (the Throne), making the phrase mean 'around the Throne'
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to form the basic noun
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific throne rather than any throne
The kasra ending shows this word is connected to what comes after it in the ayah

steps.m: to glorify, praise, or declare perfection

steps.n: Added shadda to show intensive glorification
Changes from past to present tense by adding يُ at start - shows the glorifying is happening now or regularly

steps.m: they glorify intensively (referring to the angels mentioned as الْمَلٰٓئِكَةَ)
steps.n: The plural ending ون shows this refers to the angels (الْمَلٰٓئِكَةَ) mentioned earlier who are doing the glorifying
Present tense verb meaning 'they glorify/praise' - the ون ending shows multiple angels are doing it
Changed the word from an action (to praise) into a thing (praise itself)
Added بِ to show how the angels are doing their praising. This connects to the word يُسَبِّحُونَ (they glorify) in the ayah.
Added ِ at the end because this word is connected to رَبِّهِمْ (their Lord) that comes right after it
Added fatha to first letter and doubled the middle letter to create the noun meaning 'lord'
Changed to 'my Lord' by adding the kasra sound at the end
Added pronoun suffix هِمْ meaning 'their'

steps.m: to judge or make a decision

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the past tense verb. The final ي changes to ى because it's a weak letter at the end.
Changed the vowels to make it passive voice, showing the action was done to something rather than by something

steps.m: and it was judged (connected to يُسَبِّحُونَ - they glorify)
steps.n: Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this action with the previous statement about the angels glorifying

steps.m: to be separate/clear from each other

steps.n: Adding vowels changes it from a root into a preposition meaning 'between'

steps.m: between them (referring to the angels mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added هُم at the end to show this means 'between them', referring back to the angels (الملائكة) mentioned earlier in the verse
Connected the root letters and added basic vowels to make it a past tense verb

steps.m: with/by the truth (describing how judgment was made in وَقُضِىَ)
steps.n: Added preposition ب meaning 'with/by' and final kasra. This shows how the judgment mentioned in وَقُضِىَ (it was judged) was made - with truth/justly.
Adding kasra makes it passive - something was said by someone
Final fatha completes the passive past tense form

steps.m: and it was said (connecting to قُضِىَ - it was judged)
steps.n: Added 'and' at the beginning to connect this with the previous statement about judgment (قُضِىَ) being made between them.
Changed from action to noun by adding fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject of the sentence
Added the definite article 'al' to specify this particular praise. This word is the subject of قِيلَ (it was said) in this ayah.
This is God's proper name in Arabic. The shadda shows emphasis on the ل.
Added لِ at the beginning to show this belongs to Allah or is for Allah. The ِ at the end connects it to the previous word ٱلْحَمْدُ (praise), showing that the praise belongs to Allah.

steps.m: to care for, nurture, or develop something to completion

steps.n: The doubled ب shows the complete and perfect nature of the nurturing. This creates a title for one who perfectly nurtures and sustains.
The kasra at the end shows this word connects to what comes after it (الْعٰلَمِينَ meaning 'the worlds'). Together they mean 'Lord of the worlds'.
Forms a noun meaning 'that which is known/distinguished' - referring to a realm or world
Adds plural ending to show multiple worlds/realms
Adds the definite article to specify these particular worlds/realms
Changes alif to alif with overbar for style, and adds final vowel mark. In this context, it refers to all of creation as the domains/realms that Allah has created and rules over.
